# Normalize and merge multi-head features

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate_deepseek_manual.py; do not edit. -->

This multi-head composition translation expresses mixing, normalization, and recompute stages as tile dataflow.

| --- | --- |

| Minimum thread fragment | 128 bytes |

## Core C++ kernel

```cpp title="norm_fn_pto.hpp" linenums="1"
template <typename TileVec>
inline void rsqrt_newton(TileVec &out, TileVec &a) {
    using namespace pto;
    TileVec x, t1, t2;
    TRECIP(x, a);
    for (int i = 0; i < 4; ++i) {
        TMUL(t1, x, x);
        TMUL(t2, t1, a);
        TMULS(t2, t2, -0.5f);
        TADDS(t2, t2, 1.5f);
        TMUL(x, x, t2);
    }

    TADD(out, x, x);
    TADD(out, out, x);
    (void)t1; (void)t2;
}
```

## Algorithm walkthrough

Load hidden-state and coefficient panels, compose matrix, reduction, and pointwise operations, and write the resulting head or gradient panel.

## Source-to-intrinsic mapping

[`TADD`](../../../intrinsics/tadd.md), [`TADDS`](../../../intrinsics/tadds.md), [`TCOLEXPANDMUL`](../../../intrinsics/tcolexpandmul.md), [`TLOAD`](../../../intrinsics/tload.md), [`TMUL`](../../../intrinsics/tmul.md), [`TMULS`](../../../intrinsics/tmuls.md), [`TRECIP`](../../../intrinsics/trecip.md), [`TROWEXPANDMUL`](../../../intrinsics/trowexpandmul.md), [`TROWSUM`](../../../intrinsics/trowsum.md), [`TSTORE`](../../../intrinsics/tstore.md)

Scalar C++ controls dimensions, loop bounds, and tile coordinates. Tensor
values remain in global tensor views and typed tiles throughout the data path.

## Block and thread partition

Blocks partition the outer token range; threads partition head or hidden dimensions into disjoint fragments of at least 128 bytes.

## Memory and tile flow

Global panels enter local tiles, matrix and reduction stages produce typed intermediates, and block-shared tiles are used when a value must be visible across the group.

## Build and verification

Run the migration gate from the repository root:

```console
python3 scripts/verify_deepseek_migration.py
```

The verification label records the strongest completed gate. A
`source-validated` page is a valid API example even when compiler lowering
for one of its public intrinsics is not yet available.

## Complete translated source

??? code "norm_fn_pto.hpp"

    ```cpp title="norm_fn_pto.hpp" linenums="1"
    #ifndef SUPERNPU_NORM_FN_PTO_HPP
    #define SUPERNPU_NORM_FN_PTO_HPP
    #include <common/pto_tileop.hpp>
    #include <cstddef>
    #include <cstdint>

    namespace supernpu::tile_isa {

    namespace detail {

    template <typename TileVec>
    inline void rsqrt_newton(TileVec &out, TileVec &a) {
        using namespace pto;
        TileVec x, t1, t2;
        TRECIP(x, a);
        for (int i = 0; i < 4; ++i) {
            TMUL(t1, x, x);
            TMUL(t2, t1, a);
            TMULS(t2, t2, -0.5f);
            TADDS(t2, t2, 1.5f);
            TMUL(x, x, t2);
        }

        TADD(out, x, x);
        TADD(out, out, x);
        (void)t1; (void)t2;
    }
    }

    template <int M, int N, int TileM = 16>
    void rms_norm(float *x, float *out) {
        static_assert(M > 0 && N > 0, "dim must be positive");
        static_assert(N % 8 == 0, "N must be multiple of 8");
        constexpr float kEps = 1e-6f;
        constexpr int kTM = M / TileM;
        using namespace pto;
        using gm_t = global_tensor<float, RowMajor<M, N>>;
        using tile_mat = LocalTile< float, TileM, N, BLayout::RowMajor>;
        using tile_vec = LocalTile< float, TileM, 8, BLayout::RowMajor, TileM, 1>;
        using it_t = global_iterator<gm_t, tile_mat>;
        it_t in_iter(x); it_t out_iter(out);
        const float inv_n = 1.0f / static_cast<float>(N);
        for (int tm = 0; tm < kTM; ++tm) {
            auto gi = in_iter(tm, 0); auto go = out_iter(tm, 0);
            tile_mat src, sq, dst; tile_vec sqrsum, mean, denom, rms;
            TLOAD(src, gi);
            TMUL(sq, src, src);
            TROWSUM(sqrsum, sq);
            TMULS(mean, sqrsum, inv_n);
            TADDS(denom, mean, kEps);
            detail::rsqrt_newton(rms, denom);
            TROWEXPANDMUL(dst, src, rms);
            TSTORE(go, dst);
        }
    }

    template <int M, int N, int TileM = 16, int TileN = 16>
    void fn_normw_merge_fwd(float *fn, float *normw, float *out_fn) {
        static_assert(M > 0 && N > 0, "dim must be positive");
        static_assert(TileN % 8 == 0, "TileN must be multiple of 8");
        constexpr int kTN = N / TileN;
        using namespace pto;
        using gm_m = global_tensor<float, RowMajor<M, N>>;
        using gm_v = global_tensor<float, RowMajor<1, N>>;
        using tile_mat = LocalTile< float, TileM, TileN, BLayout::RowMajor>;
        using tile_col = LocalTile< float, 1, TileN, BLayout::RowMajor, 1, TileN>;
        using it_m = global_iterator<gm_m, tile_mat>;
        using it_v = global_iterator<gm_v, tile_col>;
        it_m fn_iter(fn); it_m out_iter(out_fn); it_v nw_iter(normw);
        for (int tn = 0; tn < kTN; ++tn) {
            auto gnw = nw_iter(0, tn);
            tile_col nw; TLOAD(nw, gnw);
            for (int tm = 0; tm < M / TileM; ++tm) {
                auto gfn = fn_iter(tm, tn); auto gout = out_iter(tm, tn);
                tile_mat src, dst;
                TLOAD(src, gfn);
                TCOLEXPANDMUL(dst, src, nw);
                TSTORE(gout, dst);
            }
        }
    }

    }
    #endif
    ```
